title: The Locked Study
facts: A man is found dead alone in his study, locked from the inside.
There is a small puddle of water on the floor near the body.
The window is open, and it is winter.
best: He was killed with an icicle (or an ice weapon) that melted, leaving only water and no visible weapon. The open winter window kept the ice frozen until use.
hint: What weapon leaves a puddle but no weapon?

title: The Albatross Soup
facts: A man orders albatross soup at a restaurant, takes one taste, and immediately leaves and later takes his own life.
He had been shipwrecked years earlier and survived.
During the shipwreck he was told he was eating albatross.
best: During the shipwreck he was fed human flesh but told it was albatross. Tasting real albatross now, he realises the earlier meal was a lie — and what he actually ate.
hint: The real albatross tastes nothing like what he was served on the raft.

title: The Short Man's Elevator
facts: A man living on the 10th floor takes the elevator down every morning.
Coming home, he rides only to the 7th floor and walks the rest — except on rainy days, when he rides all the way up.
He is otherwise healthy.
best: He is very short and can only reach the button for the 7th floor. On rainy days he has an umbrella to press the 10th-floor button.
hint: What could he reach on a rainy day that he can't otherwise?

title: The Overnight Blight
facts: A farmer's field of crops is healthy one evening and dead the next morning.
Neighbouring fields are untouched.
There was no frost and no visible pest.
best: A localised chemical spill or misapplied herbicide (e.g. spray drift from the farmer's own tank, or contaminated irrigation water) hit only this field.
hint: What single thing touches only this field and not the neighbours'?

title: The Music Box
facts: A woman hears a familiar tune, then screams and collapses in grief.
She had not heard that tune in years.
Her twin died at sea long ago.
best: The tune was one only she and her twin knew; hearing it means someone who knew her twin — or her twin, thought dead — is nearby, forcing a flood of grief/recognition.
hint: Who else could possibly know that specific tune?

title: Sand in the City
facts: A colleague arrives at a downtown office with fine sand in their shoes.
There is no beach or construction site nearby.
They claim they came straight from home.
best: They did not come straight from home — they detoured somewhere with sand (a volleyball court, a children's sandbox, a gym long-jump pit, or travelled from elsewhere).
hint: The sand contradicts the 'straight from home' claim.

title: The Untouched Coffee
facts: A full, cold cup of coffee sits on a desk at 9am.
The owner is known to drink it hot, first thing, every day.
Their coat is on the chair and their car is in the lot.
best: They arrived, poured the coffee, then were interrupted or left urgently before drinking it — suggesting something unexpected pulled them away right after arrival.
hint: The coffee was poured but never drunk — when did they leave?

title: The Dog That Didn't Bark
facts: A guard dog did not bark during a nighttime theft.
The dog barks at every stranger.
Nothing was forced open.
best: The thief was someone the dog knew — an insider or the owner — not a stranger.
hint: Silence is the clue: the intruder wasn't a stranger.

title: The Dry Umbrella
facts: A visitor arrives soaked from head to toe.
They are carrying an umbrella that is completely dry.
It has been raining hard all day.
best: The umbrella is broken or they forgot to open it — or it isn't theirs and they just picked it up indoors. It provided no protection, hence they're soaked.
hint: A dry umbrella in the rain means it was never opened.

title: The Birthday Order
facts: Two babies are born to the same mother, on the same day, in the same year.
They are not twins.
This is not a mistake.
best: They are two of a set of triplets (or more) — so 'not twins' is literally true.
hint: Who says there were only two babies?

title: One Set of Footprints
facts: Fresh footprints in the snow lead up to a cabin but none lead away.
The cabin is empty.
It snowed lightly an hour ago.
best: The person walked in backwards (to fake a departure), or left before the last snowfall covered the outbound tracks, or left by another exit/vehicle.
hint: Tracks can be faked, covered, or the exit was elsewhere.

title: The Unread Messages
facts: A very responsive friend hasn't replied to messages in three days.
The messages show as delivered but not read.
Their social accounts are also silent.
best: Their phone is off or lost, or they are somewhere without signal/access (travel, hospital, retreat) — the 'delivered but unread' pattern points to no device access, not deliberate silence.
hint: Delivered-but-unread across everything suggests no access, not avoidance.

title: The Melted Groceries
facts: A neighbour's groceries, including ice cream, sit melted on their porch.
They are a reliable, organised person.
Their car is gone.
best: They set the bags down and were called away suddenly (an emergency) before bringing them in, leaving in the car in a hurry.
hint: An organised person only abandons ice cream under urgency.

title: The Stopped Clock
facts: Every clock in the house shows a different, wrong time.
The occupant is meticulous.
There was a storm last night.
best: A power cut during the storm reset the electric clocks, and they were restarted at different moments — so each shows a different offset.
hint: What single event resets clocks to blink at different times?

title: The Generous Stranger
facts: A regular customer who always tips modestly suddenly leaves a huge tip and never returns.
They seemed calm and happy that day.
They asked the server's name for the first time.
best: They were saying goodbye — moving away, retiring, or facing a life change — and chose to leave a memorable, grateful gesture on their last visit.
hint: A one-time huge tip plus 'never returns' reads as a farewell.

title: The Frame Without a Bike
facts: A bike lock is still locked to a rack, but the bike is gone.
The lock is undamaged.
Only the frame's wheels remain, locked in place.
best: The thief undid the quick-release and took the frame/body, leaving the wheels locked — or the owner locked only a wheel, not the frame. The lock secured the wrong part.
hint: What was actually locked — the frame, or just a wheel?

title: The Content Infant
facts: A usually fussy baby is suddenly calm and quiet for hours.
The parents didn't change anything they know of.
The baby feels slightly warm.
best: The baby is unwell (a fever), and lethargy from illness is being mistaken for contentment — the warmth is the tell.
hint: Unusual calm plus warmth can mean illness, not happiness.
